LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO. INC., D. JONATHAN MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, AND, ROB FORD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION REGARDING CURHAN AND FORD FOR PLAINTIFF TO SPECIFICALLY PLEAD ALLEGATIONS AGAINST THEM. AS TO THE OTHER DEFENDANTS, SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, 4TH, AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. FRAUD MUST BE PLEAD WITH SPECIFICITY AND RESULTING DAMAGES MUST BE ADEQUATELY PLEAD. AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, ALLEGE SPECIFIC PROVISIONS OF THE CORPORATIONS CODE THAT HAVE BEEN VIOLATED. OVERRULED AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (UNJUST ENRICHMENT.) (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

Off Calendar Jan-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF HENRY KHACHATURIAN MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-497034, CGC-10-503088 WITH CASE CGC-10-495904. Appearances: Matthew W. O'Reilly of O'Reilly Collins (650.358.5901) for plaintiff and moving party; Michael J. Quinn of K & L Gates (310.552.5000) for defendant and responding party. Argued and the court took the matter under submission. Parties to provide the court with a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN HOLDINGS, INC., FORMERLY KNOWN AS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CAPITAL, INC., FORMERLY KNOWN AS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO., INC., D. JONATHAN MERRIMAN, GREGORY S. CURHAN, AND ROB FORD'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.

APPEARANCES: Matthew W. O'Reilly, Esq. of O'Reilly Collins 650/358-3491 for pltf & opposing party; Christina N. Goodrich, Esq. of K&L Gates LLP 310/552-5547 for defts & moving parties (counsel noted that both defts Gregory S. Curhan & Rob Ford have been dismissed, therefore not appearing for them).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - overruled as to all counts - except as to the third cause of action, plaintiff to amend to specify recission rather than actual damages. Plaintiff to amend by 01/21/11. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN HOLDINGS, INC., FORMERLY KNOWN AS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CAPITAL, INC., FORMERLY KNOWN AS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO., INC., D. JONATHAN MERRIMAN, GREGORY S. CURHAN, AND ROB FORD'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Matthew W. O'Reilly, Esq. of O'Reilly Collins 650/358-3491 for pltf & opposing party; Christina N. Goodrich, Esq. of K&L Gates LLP 310/552-5547 for defts & moving parties (counsel noted that both defts Gregory S. Curhan & Rob Ford have been dismissed, therefore not appearing for them).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled fraud allegations sufficient for a prayer of punitive damages. Both plaintiff's and defendants' objections to requests for judicial notice are sustained. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO., INC., D. JONATHAN MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, and, ROB FORD MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar, as moot in view of court's ruling on demurrer. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO. INC., D. JONATHAN MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, and, ROB FORD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to all causes of action regarding Curhan and Ford for plaintiff to specifically plead allegations against them. As to the other defendants, sustained with leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 5th causes of action. Fraud must be plead with specificity and resulting damages must be adequately plead. As to 3rd cause of action, allege specific provisions of the Corporations Code that have been violated. Overruled as to 6th cause of action (unjust enrichment.) (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration;
Off Calendar Apr-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO., INC., PERSHING LLC, D. MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, and, ROB FORD Hearing on Petition to Compel Arbitration. Appearances: John P. Kristensen, Esq. Attorney at Law, (650) 358-5901 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Ramiz I. Rafeedie, Esq. of K&L Gates (310) 552-5000, appearing for defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to compel arbitration is denied. Agreement between Plaintiff and Pershing lacks characteristics of agreement in Macauley vs. Norlander (1992) 12 Cal.App. 4th 1. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/AJR) Judge: A. James Robertson; Court Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR # 8620
